validation_context.validation: Required DAK identifier. Use as the primary receipt/context key for the third party payment bill. Missing DAK means bill details are invalid.
DAK list number. Useful for receipt traceability; should be present for a valid DAK entry.
DAK list date. Useful for receipt chronology; should be present for a valid DAK entry.
Reference number from submitting authority/vendor. Use as alternate source document identifier when bill number is absent in generated payment text.
Reference date. Should not be a future date when used as document evidence.
Subject/description of claim. Use for semantic context of payment purpose.
Required for normal third party bills. Used for duplicate and payment narration checks.
Required. Invoice date must not be after bill date; bill date must not be a future date.
DAK captured amount. Compare with bill amount fields for anomaly/context checks.
DAK accounting month/year context.
DAK validity/status context. Prefer valid/current DAK records for payment context.
DAK creation timestamp. Use for recency and workflow chronology.
DAK unit reference. Controller syncs this from bill unit when bill is saved.
DAK vendor reference. Controller syncs this from bill vendor when bill is saved.
Vendor MSME category copied from bill MSME value during save.
Vendor make/procurement category copied from bill make type during save.
Expenditure category, usually C for capital or R for revenue, inferred from capital id/code head. Used for TPP approval power slab.

FIS document number copied from bill when valid. Duplicate FIS doc numbers in processed/pending bills are invalid; FIS doc numbers starting with 29 indicate GEM online and should not be processed as normal TPP.
FIS document date copied from bill when FIS document number is accepted.
Section foreign key. Must point to a TPP section for third party payment context.
Bill unit foreign key. Required for unit context; must match linked supply order unit when supply order exists.
Payment unit for split payment/ECHS-style cases. Used with unit paid amount where applicable.
Bill vendor foreign key. Required for third party vendor payments where `payment_record_type = 'V'`. Must match vendor in linked supply order or contract agreement.
Selected beneficiary bank/PAN detail. Required when `payment_mode = 'CMP'` and bank details are mandatory.
Required. Missing bill type makes the bill invalid.
Required. `V` means payment to vendor/third party; vendor must be selected. RATION bills must not use inappropriate vendor payment type.
Required for non-RATION bills. Valid known values: `C` CFA inherent powers, `I` IFA concurred.
Required. `CMP` requires selected beneficiary bank details; `CHQ` is invalid if vendor has valid bank account details.
Required when vendor exists. Values used in query: `1` Defence PSU, `2` PSU, `3` Startup, `4` Raksha Ratnam, `5` Private, `6` Foreign Procurement DBT, `7` Foreign Procurement LC.
Required when vendor exists. Values used in query: `1` MSME, `9` Non-MSME.
Required. If `Y`, GST booking component should exist unless SS imprest applies.
Claim amount. Used in duplicate payment key with vendor, invoice number, invoice date, office, and current valid bill exclusion.
Passed amount. Must not exceed linked supply order/contract balance. In payment validation it must equal CDA paid amount minus disallowance for vendor payment flow.
Disallowance amount. Used in amount-passed calculation.
Recovery amount. Amount payable is calculated as amount passed minus recoveries.
Principal amount considered for tax recovery context.
If true, GST-applicable bill may bypass the no-GST-booking invalidation.
Unit paid percentage for split-payment context.
Unit/payment-unit amount. For split cases, CDA paid amount plus unit paid amount must equal amount payable.
CDA paid percentage for split-payment context.
CDA/parent-unit amount. For vendor payment validation, amount passed is checked against this value less disallowance.
Required for most non-RATION third party bills. Duplicate payment check uses vendor, invoice number, invoice date, office, and amount claimed.
Required for most non-RATION third party bills. Must not be after bill date and must not be future dated.
Disbursement voucher number/payment completion context.
Disbursement voucher month.
DP sheet number for payment/accounting output context.
DP sheet date.
CMP payment date.
CMP batch identifier.
Free-text remarks/reason context.
Bill status. Import query uses `V`; validation sets `V` when valid and `I` when invalid.
Passed flag for workflow/payment state.
Cancelled flag. Cancelled bills should not be treated as payable.
Approval flag. Import query uses approved bills only.
Bill creation timestamp. Use for ordering and recency.
Validation failure/success reason stored by controller. Useful as model label/context.
Required for R&D organization bills. Also used in budget/code-head context.
Capital identifier. If present, expenditure category is capital and affects approval designation for TPP.
Must be unique among pending/valid bills for another DAK. Prefix `29` means GEM online bill and invalid for normal TPP processing.
FIS document date. Synced to DAK when FIS document number is accepted.
Required for most vendor TPP bills unless contract agreement exists or bill type/special category allows skip.
Required for most vendor TPP bills if no supply order is linked, unless bill type/special category allows skip.
Links cheque/CMP/ECS payment slip rows to the DAK.
Payment narration generated from bill type and bill/reference details. Useful for semantic payment context.
Cheque slip/payment amount. Aggregate valid rows to compare with payment amount context.
Payment slip date. Latest date gives payment event chronology.
Cheque slip beneficiary. Compare with bill selected beneficiary where present.
Cheque slip vendor for vendor payment rows. Compare with bill vendor where present.
Favour bank selected from beneficiary IFSC or vendor payment context.
Payment account number on cheque slip. Compare with beneficiary account where present.
ECS/CMP/payment mode reference. Should align with bill payment mode.
Cheque slip approval state.
Import query aggregates only `V` cheque slips.
Links PM rows to the DAK.
PM detailed code head. Used for accounting and capital/revenue inference.
Use `+C` rows for charged amount aggregation in the import query.
PM charged amount. Aggregate valid `+C` rows for charged amount context.
Latest PM date gives accounting event chronology.
Prefer active/valid PM rows for payment context.
